name: Cytology
description: Test your fundamental knowledge of cells, the basic units of life.

questions.question: What is the basic structural and functional unit of all known living organisms?
questions.explain: The cell is considered the fundamental unit of life because all living organisms are composed of cells and all cells arise from pre-existing cells.

questions.question: Which part of the cell is often called the 'control center' because it contains the genetic material?
questions.explain: The nucleus houses the cell's chromosomes, which contain DNA, making it the control center for cell activities.

questions.question: What is the primary function of mitochondria in a cell?
questions.explain: Mitochondria are known as the powerhouses of the cell, generating most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), used as a source of chemical energy.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for synthesizing proteins?
questions.explain: Ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis, translating the genetic code carried by messenger RNA (mRNA) into a specific sequence of amino acids.

questions.question: What is the jelly-like substance that fills the cell and surrounds the organelles?
questions.explain: Cytosol is the aqueous component of the cytoplasm within which various organelles and particles are suspended.

questions.question: Which type of cell lacks a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles?
questions.explain: Prokaryotic cells, like bacteria, are simpler and lack a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles found in eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: What is the main function of the cell membrane?
options: Control the passage of substances into and out of the cell
questions.explain: The cell membrane acts as a selective barrier, regulating what enters and exits the cell.

questions.question: Which organelle is involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids for secretion or delivery to other organelles?
questions.explain: The Golgi apparatus acts like a post office, processing and packaging proteins and lipids synthesized in the endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between plant and animal cells regarding structure?
options: Animal cells have a cell wall, plant cells do not
Plant cells have chloroplasts, animal cells do not
Animal cells have a nucleus, plant cells do not
Plant cells have ribosomes, animal cells do not
questions.explain: Plant cells typically possess a rigid cell wall and chloroplasts for photosynthesis, which are absent in animal cells.

questions.question: What process do plant cells use to convert light energy into chemical energy?
questions.explain: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the help of chlorophyll.
